items.summary: Audit the backend Motoko actor in backend/main.mo and verify that getAllPoetry, getAllDua, and getAllSongs (or their equivalent query functions) have correct function signatures, return types, and can be called without trapping or rejecting. Ensure each function returns an array of the correct record type with all expected fields (id, title, content, category, likeCount, and any media blob fields).

Fix the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs React Query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts. Ensure each hook calls the correct backend actor method name exactly as defined in the Motoko actor, uses a stable query key, and correctly transforms the backend response (mapping Motoko record field names to frontend TypeScript type field names). Add graceful error handling so a failed fetch shows a user-friendly error state rather than crashing the UI.

Verify and fix the PoetryPage, DuaPage, and SongsPage components to ensure their loading skeleton states, error states, and empty states are triggered correctly based on the actual data shapes returned by the fixed hooks. Remove any code that incorrectly maps field names or makes incorrect type assumptions that could cause a valid response to be treated as an error.
